title: Ghost & Residual Activity
description: Signs that a location has a persistent presence — the texture of haunting, from minor residue to significant entity activity.

entries.result: A specific smell with no source — cigarette smoke, perfume, cooking food, or something less identifiable — that appears and fades without pattern.
Temperatures that localize to specific spots and shift. The cold spots move.
A sound in an empty room that stops exactly when the door opens.
Objects found not where they were left, moved to places they belong rather than places they were.
A mirror that shows the room as it was at a different time rather than as it is.
The feeling of being watched that concentrates in one corner and moves when approached.
Writing appearing on surfaces — not threatening, not a message, just handwriting, as if someone is continuing something.
A door that opens to a different room than expected, inconsistently, only for certain people.
A pattern of small failures — equipment stops working, locks stick, lights flicker — concentrated around one person's presence.
A figure visible in peripheral vision that is absent when looked at directly.
A consistent and specific sound — footsteps, something being dragged, music — at the same time each night.
Dreams that are too specific to be coincidental and that more than one occupant of the space shares.
A room where clocks lose time consistently, regardless of mechanism.
A presence that responds to speech — not intelligently, but reactively — moving, making sound, changing the temperature.
Photographs taken in the space that include a figure not present when the picture was taken.
A presence that is clearly trying to communicate and has not yet found a method that works.
A section of floor that sounds hollow when walked over even though the building's plans show solid structure below.
A complete absence of ambient sound in one room — no street noise, no settling of the building, no wind — that feels applied rather than architectural.
A presence that is attached to a specific object rather than the location. Moving the object moves the activity.

A presence that has been here a very long time and is no longer distinguishable from the building itself. The building behaves as if it is alive, subtly, in ways that could all be explained — but accumulate.
Animals refusing to enter a specific room.
A shadow cast in the wrong direction relative to the available light.
The sense of a recent presence — warmth on a chair, a drink that has been moved — in a room that has been locked.
A presence that appears only to one member of the crew and is not detectable by any means the others use.
An old conversation, faintly, as if the room is replaying something recorded in its walls.
The sound of breathing from a closet or small space that is, when checked, empty.
A presence that becomes more substantial the more people are in the room.
A presence that becomes less substantial the more people are in the room, as if diluted by witnesses.
Something that needs to finish what it was doing before it was interrupted. It keeps trying. It cannot.
A complete and detailed record of events that took place here, replaying without the awareness of the crew, occupying the same space.
A presence that responds to names — becomes more active when specific names are spoken, becomes still when others are.
A feeling that the room is larger than it appears to be, that there's a dimension to it not captured by the walls.
Plants in the space dying rapidly and specifically — not from neglect, from something else.
A presence that seems confused about the current era and reacts to the crew as if they are someone from the location's past.
An entity trying to warn the crew about something and unable to make the content of the warning clear.
A presence that only manifests during silence and stops the moment anyone makes a sound.
The location's history arriving in fragments — smells, sounds, sensations — at irregular intervals.
A presence that has incorporated objects from its life and occasionally presents them as if making an offering.
Something that has been in this location so long it no longer has the shape it started with and has become something different.
Absolute quiet. No residue, no temperature variation, no sensations. The kind of clean absence that a practitioner would recognize as deliberate.
